column9: No new builds <$1M. 8+ month launch timeline. Gated — requires Commure exec approval. Epic negotiates integration terms directly.
column13: Epic Hyperdrive / Hyperspace. Up to 5 SmartSections for note writeback.
column14: Epic SMART on FHIR app approval, IT sandbox access, FHIR endpoint confirmed, test patient + appointment
column16: Note writeback limited to 5 SmartSections. Ambient is faster path than RCM on Epic — lead with Ambient if Epic is the EHR.
column19: Epic + MEDITECH Expanse are the only Tier 1 Agents targets. Full denial automation available.
column21: Pro is not applicable for Epic. Epic handles charge capture natively.
column22: "Our IT team controls Epic access and won't grant API credentials"
column23: Escalate to AE + exec sponsor. Epic integration requires health system IT governance approval — not a technical fix.
column24: "We want RCM and Ambient on Epic"
column25: Ambient is fastest path — FHIR API makes it straightforward. RCM requires separate HL7 build and exec approval. Sequence Ambient first, RCM in Phase 2.
column26: Lead with Ambient AI. RCM on Epic = exec conversation, not SE conversation. Agents available but position after contract.
column27: INTERNAL: Epic RCM = $1M+ deals only. Flag to AE immediately if customer brings up Epic RCM without budget signal.
column28: RCM Notion CSV, EHR One-Pager, Deal Desk 3/13

column9: Gold path. V2 API supports pull appointments, sectioned note writeback (HPI, ROS, PE, AP), E/M codes, clinical impressions. RCM Agent fully supported. MEDITECH co-sells — 15% rev share on API use.
column13: Appointment pull via V2 API — decoupled from writeback. REST 3.0 server + IdP for SMART-on-FHIR OAuth required.
column14: Expanse V2 version confirmed (2.2.58+ / v2.1.70+), REST 3.0 server, IdP for SMART-on-FHIR OAuth, dedicated IT resource for weekly API testing calls
column16: Best integration path for both RCM and Ambient. MEDITECH committed engineering resources at no cost.
column19: Full Denial Agent. Pulls 835 CARC/RARC, retrieves claim context via FHIR, writes back via HL7 DFT^P03.
column21: Do NOT lead with Pro on Expanse V2. MEDITECH co-sell relationship takes priority. Consult Vish/Ian before positioning Pro.
column22: "Our IT team isn't ready to support weekly API testing calls"
column23: Slow path: Colossus (Selenium-based) as interim while API is configured. Adds 4–6 weeks but unblocks go-live.
column24: "We're on Expanse but not sure if V2"
column25: Ask: "What version of MEDITECH Expanse are you running? We need to confirm 2.2.58+ or v2.1.70+." If unclear, ask IT to pull version string. Magic/C/S = completely different path.
column26: Confirm version BEFORE scoping. Version is the fork in the road. One question saves the entire discovery.
column27: INTERNAL: MEDITECH is P0 partner. Do not undermine co-sell by leading with Pro on Expanse V2. Alert AE to co-sell opportunity — 15% rev share.
column28: MEDITECH RCM Agents doc, Deal Desk 3/13, Expanse Integration Options

column9: Standard Expanse path. V2 API not available at this version. Colossus handles browser automation. RCM Agent available with limitations.
column13: Appointment pull via Colossus is browser-based — Justin's note: treat as Selenium for scoping purposes. DOM writeback NOT used here — HL7 inbound means appts are tied to writeback flow.
column14: MEDITECH Expanse version confirmed, NPR access (if HL7), Colossus infrastructure, test environment, IT point of contact
column16: Upgrade path to V2 should be part of conversation — position V2 as the long-term architecture.
column19: Agents available. Writeback limitations compared to V2. Confirm scope with engineering.
column21: Consult Vish/Ian before positioning Pro. Same co-sell consideration as V2.
column23: HL7 NMI path if available. Longer timeline (add 4–6 weeks) but no browser dependency.
column24: "Can we upgrade to Expanse V2?"
column25: Yes — and it unlocks the full API path for both RCM and Ambient. Position upgrade as a value enabler, not a requirement.
column26: Ask about version in first discovery call. Qualifying V2 vs standard Expanse changes the entire scoping conversation.
column27: INTERNAL: Same co-sell rules as V2. Do not lead with Pro.
column28: MEDITECH RCM Agents doc, Expanse Integration Options doc

column9: Legacy platform. Deep integration via custom NPR code for outbound data, HL7 for inbound. No FHIR APIs. Commure Pro fills the gap for charge capture.
column13: Appointment pull via custom NPR scripting — browser-based equivalent. Tied to writeback infrastructure.
column14: NPR database access, HL7 NMI interface access, IT resources familiar with Magic NPR, test environment
column16: Legacy architecture. Every Magic site is different. Scope carefully.
column19: Agents not available on Magic. No FHIR APIs. Do not position Agents.
column21: Commure Pro (PatientKeeper) is the right conversation for Magic clients. Autonomous Coding + Charge Capture for hospitalist/inpatient. Lead with Pro opportunity — flag to Pro team immediately.
column22: "Our NPR team is unavailable for this project"
column23: Without NPR access, Magic integration is blocked. Explore upgrade to C/S or Expanse as an alternative path.
column24: "We don't want to do the upgrade right now"
column25: Commure Pro is available today without the upgrade. Position as value-now vs. value-later when they're ready for Expanse.
column26: Flag Pro opportunity immediately. Escalate to Pro team (Vish/Ian). Do not scope as standard RCM Agents deal.
column27: INTERNAL: Magic = Pro opportunity. Do not scope standard Agents. Pro team owns this conversation after your handoff.
column28: MEDITECH Overview deck, Commure Pro deck, Deal Desk 3/13

column9: Similar to Magic but slightly newer architecture. Same NPR dependency. Commure Pro applicable. No FHIR APIs.
column13: Same as Magic — appointment pull tied to NPR scripting.

column16: Same constraints as Magic. Pro conversation should happen in parallel.

column9: Athena is gating all practices — causing access issues. First HL7 via AZ Urology potentially happening. Replaces Athena Collector.
column13: Athena API: authorization via marketplace.athenahealth.com. Requires Augmedix authorization consent form. 2FA must be disabled for service accounts. Appointment Report Builder access required.
column14: Athena API authorization form completed, 2FA disabled for Commure service accounts, Appointment Report Builder access, test patient + appointment
column16: API path is cleaner and faster for Ambient. Selenium fallback available but adds complexity and gating risk.
column19: Agents not currently available for Athena.
column22: "Our Athena admin won't disable 2FA for service accounts"
column23: Selenium path as fallback — does not require API credentials. Note: Selenium on Athena has the gating risk (Athena can restrict access). Document the risk.
column24: "We need appointment syncing automated"
column25: API path handles this natively. If API auth is blocked, Selenium pulls appointments browser-based (tied to writeback). If DOM writeback, appointments are decoupled — can use CSV (1–2 days) or HL7.
column26: Confirm API authorization status in first call. Gating is a real risk — have the Selenium fallback ready to present.
column27: INTERNAL: Athena gating is an active issue. Check with engineering on current gate status before committing timeline.
column28: Athena SOW, Integration Scope Template, RCM Notion CSV

column9: Selenium pulls capped at 1 DOS per pull — use HL7 for larger sites (e.g., Pediatrics Associates of Charlottesville). One of easiest paths.
column12: DOM = no appt sync needed. Selenium = browser pull.
column13: If DOM writeback: appointment sync NOT required — providers manually add patients or use self-serve workflow. If Selenium writeback: browser-based appt pull included.
column14: EHR login credentials (3 sets), test patient, test appointment, note access permission
column16: eCW supports Orders integration (Imaging, Labs, Procedures, Medications) via structured entry — strong differentiator. ICD-10 + CPT available.
column22: "We need appointment syncing automated but don't want browser automation"
column23: If using DOM writeback, appointments are decoupled. Options: (1) CSV feed — can be done in 1–2 days, (2) HL7 — more robust, future-compatible, preferred for larger sites.
column24: "Selenium pulls at 1 DOS per pull are too slow for our patient volume"
column25: Switch to HL7 for appointment ingestion. Adds 2–4 weeks but handles high-volume sites reliably. Already proven (Pediatrics Associates of Charlottesville).
column26: Ask about patient volume in discovery. >500 appointments/day = HL7 path. Lead with DOM for fastest Ambient go-live.
column27: INTERNAL: DOM + manual appts = fastest Ambient go-live. If they need appt sync, add HL7 as Phase 2 or price into scope.
column28: RCM Notion CSV, Integration Scope Template, eCW SOW templates, Deal Desk 3/13

column9: 837 batch file integration. ~6 month launch timeline. No new builds <$500K.
